answer:
### Manusmṛti's Account of the Four Āśramas

The *Manusmṛti* devotes Chapter 6 almost entirely to the āśramas (stages of life), beginning with the transition from householder (*gṛhastha*) to forest-dweller (*vānaprastha*), then to ascetic (*bhikṣu* or *sannyāsin*). The four are explicitly named as celibate student (*brahmacārin*), householder, forest-dweller, and ascetic, all originating from and supported by the householder.[^1] The chapter emphasizes sequential progression after fulfilling debts (study, progeny, sacrifice), with the householder as foundational.[^2]

- **Celibate Student (*Brahmacārin*)**: Assumed prior (detailed in Ch. 2); transitions to householder after *snāna* (bathing rite).[^3]
- **Householder (*Gṛhastha*)**: Second quarter of life; performs five great sacrifices, begets sons, supports others.[^4]
- **Forest-Dweller (*Vānaprastha*)**: Third quarter ("third part of his life"); departs village upon seeing wrinkles/gray hair/child's child, lives on roots/fruits, maintains fires initially, practices austerities.[^5]
- **Ascetic (*Sannyāsin*)**: Fourth quarter ("fourth part of his life"); renounces fires/objects, wanders alone begging alms, contemplates liberation (*mokṣa*); highest state via non-attachment.[^6]

Life is divided into quarters, with clear temporal boundaries tied to lifespan progression.[^7]

### Yājñavalkya Smṛti's Account of the Four Āśramas

The *Yājñavalkya Smṛti* treats the āśramas sequentially across chapters: student and householder in Ch. 1, forest-dweller and ascetic in Ch. 3 (under expiation/duties). The sequence mirrors Manu (student → householder → forest-dweller → ascetic), but duties are briefer, integrated into broader *dharma*.[^8]

- **Celibate Student (*Brahmacārin*)**: 8th year (Brāhmaṇa) from conception/birth; studies Veda, begs alms, avoids meat/honey; ends ~16th year with *keśānta*.[^9]
- **Householder (*Gṛhastha*)**: Daily *mahāyajñas*, hospitality, protects dependents; no explicit duration.[^10]
- **Forest-Dweller (*Vānaprastha*)**: Entrusts wife to sons (or takes her); unploughed foods, three daily baths, austerities (five fires in summer, etc.); collections discarded in Āśvayuja.[^11]
- **Ascetic (*Yati*)**: After *prājāpatya* sacrifice from house/forest; wanders with staff/pot, begs 8 mouthfuls, controls senses; contemplates body/transmigration.[^12]

No explicit lifespan quarters; progression after debts/rites, like Manu.

### Comparison of Boundaries

Boundaries align: same sequence without shift. Manu explicitly divides life into four parts (e.g., "third part" forest, "fourth part" ascetic), tying to physical signs (wrinkles, grandchildren).[^13] Yājñavalkya implies sequence via transitions (e.g., *upanayana* ages, post-sacrifice renunciation) but omits proportional lifespan divisions—no "quarters" or "third/fourth part." No evidence Yājñavalkya "shifts" them; it condenses without altering order or prerequisites.[^14]

[^1]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 6, [p-59814f](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-59814f).  
[^2]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 6, [p-a6e55e](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-a6e55e).  
[^3]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 6, [p-89d810](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-89d810) (contextual transition).  
[^4]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 6, [p-ed3d57](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-ed3d57).  
[^5]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 6, [p-89d810](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-89d810) to [p-058b16](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-058b16).  
[^6]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 6, [p-ed3d57](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-ed3d57).  
[^7]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 6, [whole chapter](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/).  
[^8]: *Yājñavalkya Smṛti*, Ch. 3, [p-d4adaa](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-d4adaa).  
[^9]: *Yājñavalkya Smṛti*, Ch. 1, [p-546db5](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-546db5).  
[^10]: *Yājñavalkya Smṛti*, Ch. 1, [p-a80877](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-a80877).  
[^11]: *Yājñavalkya Smṛti*, Ch. 3, [p-d4adaa](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-d4adaa).  
[^12]: *Yājñavalkya Smṛti*, Ch. 3, [p-eaf75a](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-eaf75a).  
[^13]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 6, [p-89d810](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-89d810).  
[^14]: *Yājñavalkya Smṛti*, Ch. 3, [whole āśrama sections](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/).
